description:
You are helping a life-science researcher who is reorganising several small text datasets stored in their
    home directory.  
    The researcher created three source files but needs a single, neatly-aligned table that pulls specific
    columns out of each.  Because other software will later check the result automatically, it is vital that
    the exact file paths, column order, delimiter choice and header line match the specification below
    byte-for-byte.

    1.  Source material  
        • /home/user/datasets/experiment_A.csv  
        • /home/user/datasets/experiment_B.csv  
        • /home/user/datasets/notes.tsv  

        All three files already exist when you start the task.  
        • experiment_A.csv is comma-separated.  
        • experiment_B.csv is comma-separated.  
        • notes.tsv is tab-separated.  

    2.  Columns the researcher needs  
        • From experiment_A.csv → column 1 (SampleID) **and** column 3 (GeneCount)  
        • From experiment_B.csv → column 2 (Expression)  
        • From notes.tsv       → column 4 (Remark)

    3.  Required output  
        Create the directory /home/user/results (if it does not yet exist) and inside it create a file called
        /home/user/results/combined_dataset.tsv that satisfies every rule below:

        a.  It must be TAB-separated (not spaces, not commas).  
        b.  The very first line is an exact header:  
            SampleID<TAB>GeneCount<TAB>Expression<TAB>Remark  
        c.  Lines 2-6 contain the rows taken from the inputs **in their original order** (no sorting, no
            re-ordering).  The automated test expects precisely five data rows.  
        d.  Each line, including the last one, ends with a single UNIX newline character (LF).  
        e.  There must be no extra blank lines anywhere in the file.

    4.  Logging requirement  
        For reproducibility, write a plain-text file called  
        /home/user/results/process.log  
        capturing *every* shell command you actually executed (one command per line, in the order you ran
        them).  The test harness will merely check that the file exists and contains at least four
        non-empty lines; the exact commands can vary, so do not worry about matching any reference text.
        Make sure the log file ends with a newline so it is POSIX-compliant.

    5.  Hints (you are free to ignore)  
        • cut and paste are well-suited when column numbers rather than names are needed.  
        • When mixing comma- and tab-separated sources you can first “cut” each piece separately, then
          “paste ‑d '\t' ...” them together.  
        • You do not need root privileges at any stage.

    The task is finished when BOTH /home/user/results/combined_dataset.tsv and
    /home/user/results/process.log exist and the contents of combined_dataset.tsv perfectly match the
    four-column, six-line, tab-delimited layout described above.
truth:
<!--
        Ground truth for automated assessment
        --------------------------------------
        1. The three source files are pre-populated with the exact texts below.
    -->
    <source_files>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/experiment_A.csv"><![CDATA[
SampleID,Condition,GeneCount,Quality
S1,control,1523,0.98
S2,treated,1480,0.95
S3,control,1601,0.97
S4,treated,1499,0.96
S5,control,1588,0.99
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/datasets/experiment_B.csv"><![CDATA[
ID,Expression,Dose
S1,0.83,5
S2,0.72,10
S3,0.79,5
S4,0.75,10
S5,0.81,5
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/datasets/notes.tsv"><![CDATA[
Run	Date	Operator	Remark
1	2023-03-13	Dr.X	OK
2	2023-03-14	Dr.X	OK
3	2023-03-15	Dr.X	Retry
4	2023-03-16	Dr.X	OK
5	2023-03-17	Dr.X	OK
]]></file>
    </source_files>

    <!--
        2. Exact target file content for verification.
           Each “␉” comment below denotes a single TAB; “␊” denotes LF.
    -->
    <expected_output_file path="/home/user/results/combined_dataset.tsv"><![CDATA[
SampleID	GeneCount	Expression	Remark
S1	1523	0.83	OK
S2	1480	0.72	OK
S3	1601	0.79	Retry
S4	1499	0.75	OK
S5	1588	0.81	OK
]]></expected_output_file>

    <!--
        3. The process.log file only needs to exist and contain ≥4 non-empty lines;
           its exact content is intentionally *not* fixed.
    -->
    <log_file_requirements>
        <file path="/home/user/results/process.log" must_exist="yes" min_non_empty_lines="4"/>
    </log_file_requirements>
